title: skopeo copy --preserve-digests does not preserve digest
body:
We are trying to copy an image from Artifactory to ECR, preserving the SHA-256 digest.

```
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.175Z] + skopeo copy --preserve-digests --insecure-policy --src-creds **** --retry-times 3 docker://[artifactory]/ingress-nginx/controller@sha256:4ba73c697770664c1e00e9f968de14e08f606ff961c76e5d7033a4a9c593c629 docker://[account].dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/[redacted]/ingress-nginx/controller:v1.5.1
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.433Z] Getting image source signatures
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.433Z] Copying blob sha256:4f4fb700ef54461cfa02571ae0db9a0dc1e0cdb5577484a6d75e68dc38e8acc1
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.433Z] Copying blob sha256:0acd6e4f55d7131d496583d5620fa7634bf3804b0ec61d8e9d8a9619f91c8d78
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.433Z] Copying blob sha256:213ec9aee27d8be045c6a92b7eac22c9a64b44558193775a1a7f626352392b49
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.433Z] Copying blob sha256:a8fbdcfedb328e87da816a8739cde98b2b0e7d353efdecd121f27630a6876950
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.433Z] Copying blob sha256:cc460fb2cb85828e654117c123bec6759d5d240b41e79bf557977a347e4a9689
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.433Z] Copying blob sha256:08610480b3bb67a814d42e4fe977edb2e6690a4b961ec419e0f5d17f04b95dd2
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.433Z] Copying blob sha256:8280fde6001d50831435ec05d7138734155547760271de0bbaceda9c7be353e9
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.433Z] Copying blob sha256:73de260e9899b63ac9658f1d11fad3c0fd42d5c7f93a99273e93cb7d5733a70c
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.433Z] Copying blob sha256:230441d2bc1a61e0fa173a21e07bfd9d31de115b463636267b517ea69b1bffcb
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.433Z] Copying blob sha256:a4eac395574239077f75cea8353fbfa02150a09543cc32327e24f66803534fea
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.433Z] Copying blob sha256:0a8d00bc8d6ed1819b3f3c00a8d8aa9ab7c192f9c80e57625a503a529d797fe4
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.433Z] Copying blob sha256:0d06e89c7347793bdbf6c4d1198c5c2e1468702cec7c454533919f48645081b2
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.689Z] Copying blob sha256:58e31f86fa09a39bbc32c59321f8395abeb20f7cd8e04bfdd69291f2a8a58489
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.689Z] Copying blob sha256:e089bf3561172902bd89f354cacfd26d8b654566ab67b63e1417e7b9aab3477a
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.689Z] Copying config sha256:f2e1146a6d96ac8eebb251284f45f8569f5879c6ec894ae1335d26617d36af2d
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.689Z] Writing manifest to image destination
[2023-03-10T14:16:35.947Z] Storing signatures
```

However, according to ECR the image digest is sha256:2f7551977e8553a50cd88e8175b1411acbef319f7040357b58be95e9b99c07e5.

We are using skopeo 1.11.1.

My guess is that the ingress-nginx image is multi-platform, and since we are not passing `--all` it is only copying the linux/amd64 version. But in that case `skopeo copy` should fail, as the docs claim.

On that note, it is unclear what the difference is between `--all` and `--multi-arch all`.

comments.body:
> My guess is that the ingress-nginx image is multi-platform, and since we are not passing `--all` it is only copying the linux/amd64 version.

Yes.

> But in that case `skopeo copy` should fail, as the docs claim.

That would be a reasonable way to implement it; for better or worse, the current behavior explicitly documented to be this way, per the man page

> Preserve the digests during copying. Fail if the digest cannot be preserved. Consider using `--all` at the same time.

---

> On that note, it is unclear what the difference is between `--all` and `--multi-arch all`.

There isn’t any (and using both simultaneously is rejected). The `--multi-arch` format allows expressing more alternatives (and even more might be added in the future.)

The statement in the docs is a bit ambiguous. We were interpreting it to include failures to preserve the digest of the source image itself. But I guess it is only counting the digests of the images it chooses to copy in the first place.

Maybe the docs can be updated to make this more clear. It would also help to explain when `--all` (or `--multi-arch all`) is needed, as right now it just says to "consider" it without providing any details. I'm assuming the intent was that people would refer to the documentation for `--all`, but that assumes you know you are dealing with a multi-arch image, and you know that failing to "preserve" that digest doesn't count as an error.
The documentation could definitely be improved. Care to provide a PR with a suggested wording?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
